---
title: "R Episode 11 — Reading & Writing Data"
---

::: {.callout-note}
## Objectives
By the end of this episode you will be able to:

- Import tabular data with `readr`, controlling column types.
- Write results and figures to disk reproducibly.
- Separate read-only inputs from generated outputs.

**Teaching:** 25 min · **Exercises:** 20 min
:::

## Reading data with readr

```r
library(readr)
library(here)

gapminder <- read_csv(here("data", "gapminder.csv"))
```

`read_csv()` prints and guesses column types. When a guess is wrong, **be explicit** — this is a
reproducibility safeguard:

```r
gapminder <- read_csv(
  here("data", "gapminder.csv"),
  col_types = cols(year = col_integer(), country = col_character())
)
```

Other importers: `read_tsv()`, `read_delim()`, and **readxl**'s `read_excel()` for spreadsheets.

## Writing data

```r
library(dplyr)

recent <- gapminder |> filter(year >= 2000)
write_csv(recent, here("output", "gapminder_recent.csv"))
```

::: {.callout-tip}
## Instructor note
Reinforce the project discipline from Episode 2: **`data/` is read-only**, **`output/` is
generated** (and git-ignored). If you can delete `output/` and regenerate it by re-running your
code, your analysis is reproducible.
:::

## Saving figures and R objects

```r
ggplot2::ggsave(here("output", "fig1.png"), width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)

saveRDS(recent, here("output", "recent.rds"))   # exact R object
recent2 <- readRDS(here("output", "recent.rds"))
```

Use CSV for interoperability; use `.rds` when you need to preserve exact R types.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true"}
## Challenge — Round-trip
Filter `gapminder` to African countries, write it to `output/africa.csv`, then read it back and
confirm the row count matches.

### Solution

```r
africa <- gapminder |> filter(continent == "Africa")
write_csv(africa, here("output", "africa.csv"))
back <- read_csv(here("output", "africa.csv"))
nrow(africa) == nrow(back)   # TRUE
```
:::

::: {.callout-important}
## Key points
- `read_csv()` imports and reports types; set `col_types` when guesses are wrong.
- `write_csv()` for portability; `saveRDS()`/`readRDS()` to preserve R types.
- Keep `data/` read-only and `output/` regenerable — that's reproducibility.
:::

Next: [Episode 12 — Data Manipulation with dplyr](r-12-dplyr.qmd).
